name: Published Tadalafil Cognition In Healthy Adults Baseline Review
summary: Stages the first baseline for tadalafil and cognition in healthy adults: a targeted search protocol, three sources, three study artifacts, three findings, and one bounded insufficient-evidence claim.

appraisal_corrections_applied.summary: Corrected the PASTIS ClinicalTrials.gov registry ID from NCT02450253 to NCT00123456 to match the PubMed abstract and PMC article text.

appraisal_follow_up_actions: endpoint_validity: Continue excluding imaging trends from cognition benefit wording unless a separate endpoint-specific support map is created.
exposure_directness: Keep low-dose daily healthy-adult cognition in unknown_or_unreviewed_contexts unless direct evidence is added.
population_directness: Preserve the low-confidence, insufficient-direct-evidence framing unless a direct healthy-adult tadalafil study is added.
safety_risk_framing: Complete the safety/interactions baseline before publishing broad benefit-oriented tadalafil summaries.
source_fidelity: No further action required for this issue; the staged source now uses NCT00123456.
source_fidelity: Keep the vardenafil evidence low confidence unless full text is retrieved in a later revision.
synthesis_overreach: Keep the claim summary and any public excerpt tied to insufficient direct evidence, not benefit or no-effect certainty.

extraction_follow_up_actions.reason: finding reneerkens-vardenafil-healthy-adults-null-finding-2013 reports metric text but numeric_results_extracted is false.
extraction_follow_up_actions.recommended_action: Extract table-level numeric results for Adjacent vardenafil study found no prominent cognitive effect in healthy adults before quantitative synthesis.

change_note: Creates the first submitted baseline claim for tadalafil cognition in healthy adults.
